DOWNLOAD
DWSIM: The Open Source Chemical Process Simulator
Rigorous chemistry. Real engineering. Zero license fees.
DWSIM is a full-featured chemical process simulator trusted by students, researchers, and industry professionals around the world. Model steady-state and dynamic flowsheets with the same thermodynamic rigor you'd expect from commercial
                      tools — on Windows, Linux, macOS, and even Raspberry Pi.
Built for Engineers. Open to Everyone.
DWSIM combines decades of chemical engineering theory with a modern, cross-platform interface. Whether you're sizing a distillation column, optimizing a biorefinery, or teaching a thermodynamics class, DWSIM gives you the tools to
                      get the answer right — without a five-figure license.
100% Free and Open Source under the GNU GPL v3
Cross-platform — runs natively on Windows, Linux, and macOS
CAPE-OPEN compliant — no vendor lock-in
A Complete Simulation Environment
Rigorous Thermodynamics
Industry-standard property packages backed by peer-reviewed models:
Equations of State: Peng-Robinson, PRSV2, Soave-Redlich-Kwong, Lee-Kesler-Plöcker and more
Activity Coefficient Models: NRTL, UNIQUAC, Wilson, UNIFAC and more
Specialty Packages: Steam tables, CoolProp integration for pure fluids and incompressible mixtures
Petroleum Characterization: Riazi correlations, pseudo-component generation, contaminants tracking, true critical point calculation
35+ Unit Operations
Everything needed to build realistic flowsheets out of the box:
Rigorous and shortcut distillation columns
Heat exchangers, compressors, expanders, pumps, turbines, valves
CSTR, PFR, and equilibrium reactors
Pipes with detailed hydraulic and heat-transfer calculations
Logical blocks, controllers, spreadsheet blocks, and Python script operations
Steady-State and Dynamic Simulation
Switch between design-mode steady-state solving and fully transient dynamic simulation with rigorous time-stepping integrators, controllers, and event scheduling — all inside the same flowsheet.
Built-in NIST ThermoML Data Access
DWSIM now ships with direct access to the NIST ThermoML Archive — over
120,000 phase-equilibrium datasets
and thousands of pure-compound property records, available offline.
Pure Compound Importer
Search by name, CAS, or InChIKey and pull a fully-populated compound into your simulation. When the source omits a property, DWSIM fills the gap automatically with classic estimation methods (Joback, Lee-Kesler, Ambrose, Riedel, Rackett, and more)
      — so the imported compound works immediately with any property package. Every property carries its provenance: source citation or estimator name.
Binary Phase Equilibrium Data Search
Inside the Data Regression tool, search the local ThermoML index for VLE / LLE / SLE / VLLE datasets matching your compound pair. Filter by equilibrium type and T/P range, preview the citation, and load experimental x–y–T–P points straight into the
      regression grid to fit interaction parameters for
PR, SRK, PRSV2, NRTL, UNIQUAC, PC-SAFT
and Lee-Kesler-Plöcker.
Both features are reachable from the Compound Creator, Simulation Wizard, Simulation Settings and Data Regression forms.
Aqueous Electrolytes Done Right
Brines, sour gas, acid pickling, CO₂ capture, desalination, hydrometallurgy — the chemistry behind half the process industry runs on water and ions. DWSIM's Electrolytes extension ((Patreon Edition exclusive) brings that chemistry into your
            flowsheet with the same rigor you'd expect from commercial simulators, no five-figure license required.
Two industry-standard activity-coefficient models — Electrolyte NRTL (Chen & Evans) and Extended UNIQUAC (Thomsen) — drive a unified equilibrium engine that solves dissociation, salt precipitation, complexation and vapor-liquid equilibrium
            in a single pass. Atom and charge balance hold across liquid, solid and vapor. Get the speciation right the first time.
Electrolyte NRTL and Extended UNIQUAC property packages
Multi-salt precipitation with simultaneous Ksp solving
pH, ionic strength, activity coefficients and saturation indices per stream
Phase Diagram Utility — T–w, ternary, solubility-vs-temperature, activity-coefficient plots
AI-Powered Process Engineering
DWSIM brings modern AI directly into the simulation workflow — not as a gimmick, but as a genuine productivity multiplier for engineers. Ask questions, generate flowsheets, diagnose convergence issues, and accelerate your work with the built-in
                AI Assistant.
🤖 DWSIM AI Assistant
An LLM-powered assistant integrated right inside the simulator. Ask questions about your flowsheet, the thermodynamics you should be using, how to set up a unit operation, or why a calculation isn't converging — and get informed, context-aware
                answers without leaving DWSIM.
Natural-language help for every unit operation and property package
Flowsheet interpretation — explain what a simulation is doing and highlight issues
Script generation — produce Python and VB.NET snippets for automation tasks
Connect to your preferred LLM provider, including local models via an LLM server
🔍 AI Insights for Flowsheets & Unit Operations
One-click AI analysis of your entire flowsheet — or of any individual unit operation. DWSIM reads the current solution, equipment specifications, and stream data, then returns a report you can act on:
Flowsheet-level insights
— Overall process summary, mass & energy balance sanity checks, bottleneck detection, efficiency observations, and suggested improvements
Unit operation insights
— Per-equipment diagnostics (distillation column profile analysis, heat exchanger pinch evaluation, reactor conversion review, compressor performance, etc.) with targeted recommendations
Design & troubleshooting hints
— Highlight specifications that are pushing equipment limits, flag unusual conditions, and propose next steps
Report-ready output
— Generate executive summaries and engineering notes you can paste straight into reports, reviews, or class assignments
✨ AI Design Mode (Patreon Edition exclusive)
Go from idea to flowsheet in minutes. Describe the process you want — "a three-column separation train for a crude benzene-toluene-xylene mixture" — and let the AI Design Mode propose, place, and connect the unit operations for you. Review,
                tweak, and simulate.
AI Convergence Enhancer (Patreon Edition exclusive)
The AI-Assisted Convergence Enhancer (ACE) is a machine-learning extension that improves the robustness and speed of thermodynamic calculations throughout the DWSIM flowsheet solver. The underlying technology is a set of fully connected
                feedforward artificial neural networks (ANNs) trained on convergence data collected during ordinary simulations. The trained networks are used to provide either improved initial estimates or complete fallback solutions for flash calculations
                and reactor equilibria that are difficult to converge by conventional iterative methods.
AI-Assisted Parameter Optimization
Built-in tools for ADM1 anaerobic digester parameter regression and other data-fitting workflows, combining classical optimization with modern ML-backed heuristics for faster, more reliable convergence on complex nonlinear models.
✨ AI Insights for LCA & TEA Reports (Patreon Edition exclusive)
DWSIM integrates Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) and Techno-Economic Analysis (TEA) directly into the simulation environment — and AI Insights turns those numbers into actionable narratives.
LCA insights
— Automatic interpretation of environmental impact categories (GWP, acidification, eutrophication, water use, etc.), identification of the biggest contributors in your process, and suggestions for greener
                  alternatives
TEA insights
— Plain-English reading of CAPEX, OPEX, NPV, IRR, and payback metrics, with sensitivity commentary highlighting which process decisions most affect profitability
Scenario comparison
— Ask the AI to compare two flowsheet variants side-by-side on both economic and environmental fronts
Executive-ready reports
— Generate polished summaries suitable for management presentations, technical papers, or grant applications
Processes You Can Simulate with DWSIM
Oil & Gas / Petroleum Refining
Crude oil distillation (atmospheric and vacuum towers)
Natural gas processing, sweetening (amine units), and dehydration (glycol units)
LNG liquefaction and regasification
Fluid catalytic cracking feed conditioning, hydrotreating, reforming support calculations
Pipeline hydraulics, two-phase and multiphase flow
Gas compression and expansion trains
Petroleum assay characterization and blending
Petrochemicals & Chemicals
Ethylene, propylene, and olefins separation trains
Methanol, ammonia, and urea synthesis loops
Aromatic extraction and BTX separation
Chlor-alkali and electrolyte processes
Polymerization reactor feed conditioning
Acid gas treatment and sulfur recovery
Power, Energy & Sustainability
Steam cycles (Rankine, reheat, regenerative)
Gas turbine and combined-cycle performance
Heat pumps, refrigeration cycles, and ORC systems
CO2 capture (amine, physical solvent, membrane)
Hydrogen production and purification
Geothermal and waste-heat recovery
Pharmaceuticals & Specialty Chemicals
Batch reactor design and scale-up
Solvent recovery and recycling
Crystallization and solids separation
Extraction and purification trains
Education & Research
Classroom flowsheets for unit operations courses
Thermodynamics and phase-equilibria teaching
Process control and dynamics labs
Research into novel process intensification, green chemistry, and electrification
Biofuels, Biotech & Bioprocessing
Bioethanol and biodiesel production
Anaerobic digestion and biogas upgrading (ADM1-based)
Fermentation and bioreactor design
Downstream processing: chromatography, ultrafiltration, crystallization
Biorefinery flowsheets (lignocellulosic, algal, waste-to-energy)
Water, Wastewater & Environmental
Desalination (MSF, MED, RO via crossflow ultrafiltration models)
Wastewater anaerobic treatment (ADM1)
Evaporation and crystallization for brine management
VOC absorption and stripping
Food, Beverage & Consumer Products
Distillation and rectification for spirits
Evaporation, drying, and concentration
Flavor and essential oil recovery
DWSIM Works With Your Stack
Python
— Built-in IronPython console plus Python script unit operations
Excel & Office
— Native Excel add-in, property interface, and export to Office formats
CAPE-OPEN 1.0 / 1.1
— Use DWSIM models in third-party simulators, or plug third-party CAPE-OPEN unit operations and property packages into DWSIM
Python, .NET & COM Automation API
— Drive simulations programmatically from Python, C#, VB.NET, or any COM-capable language
TCP/IP Server
— Run DWSIM headlessly as a simulation backend for web apps, digital twins, or optimization loops
Project Sponsors
Simulate 365
AI4Tech
ABEQ
Macaw Energies
FOSSEE
DWSIM Patreon Edition — Exclusive to Our Supporters
Everything in the standard DWSIM release is free and open source — including the full petroleum refining and bioprocess capabilities. The
DWSIM Patreon Edition
is a special packaged build available only to
                      DWSIM Patreon supporters, delivering extra unit operations, property packages, plugins, and support perks that thank the community funding DWSIM’s ongoing development.
🥉 Explorer
A friendly first step into the Patreon Edition.
New Unit Operations
Air Cooler
Energy Stream Mapper
Material Stream Mapper
Energy Stream Switch
Energy Stream Splitter
New Property Packages
Extended CoolProp
New Extensions / Plugins
Object Explorer Plugin
Other Extras
